description.p: wdotool is a command-line input automation tool for Wayland. It does what xdotool does on X11 (synthetic keystrokes, mouse moves, clicks, scrolling, window listing and focusing) but using the protocols Wayland actually provides for this work: libei via the XDG RemoteDesktop portal, wlroots virtual-keyboard and virtual-pointer, KWin scripting on KDE, and a companion Shell extension on GNOME.
It respects compositor focus and permissions instead of bypassing them with /dev/uinput. The argv surface stays close to xdotool so existing scripts can be ported with minimal changes.

Initial Flathub-targeted release. Adds the GNOME backend paired with a companion Shell extension, libei Unicode fallback for keys outside the active layout on wlroots, and a control-character fix for the wlroots type command.
